solution: Target the local profile. Instruct the worker to log completely out of the GlobalProtect client, flush the local DNS cache via terminal ('ipconfig /flushdns'), cycle the router power, and attempt re-authentication via the nearest regional gateway.

Isolate power vs interface failure. Swap out the existing HDMI/DisplayPort cable with a tested corporate fallback asset, check the monitor's refresh rate settings in the OS Display properties panel, and perform a hard reset on the docking station.
1. Clear Windows Update cache. 2. Run 'sfc /scannow' in CMD. 3. Restart the Windows Update service.
